text:
Market today: Risk-off vibes driven by the chip rout - Samsung and SK Hynix both cratering 9%+ is the real story today. When Korean memory names get hit that hard, the whole semi complex feels it. Your kind of names (chips, tech ETFs) are directly in the crosshairs.

Your holdings: SOXL (-14.52%) is the urgent one - leveraged 3X semi exposure on margin means you're feeling this chip selloff triple. Stop is at $200, price is $217, but that gap closes fast in a rout. SPAL (-13.72%) is a brand-new, very volatile leveraged ETF - it's already down nearly 14% and your stop at $21.38 is basically right here at $21.70; any tick down triggers it. SMH (-2.5%) is your cleaner semi read - also sliding, worth watching as a canary.

Watch: Whether the chip rout deepens on Monday following the Korean names. Also watch SOXL's $200 stop - margin amplifies every dollar it drops.

Bottom line: Protect capital on your leveraged positions right now - this chip news is not your friend today.
plays: SOXL: Stop at $200 is your line - with the Korean chip rout spreading, consider manually tightening or reducing size now before it hits. Margin makes this expensive to be wrong.
SPAL: At $21.70 with a $21.38 stop, you're essentially at the exit already - confirm your stop is live; don't let a fast-moving leveraged ETF slip past it.
No new buys today - chip sector under real pressure; sitting tight beats forcing a long into a rout.
